page.content: In the Vodafone Group's 2Q FY2025 report, the IoT connection growth metrics indicate that in Q1 FY2025, there were 12M connections generating revenue of $90M, while in Q2 FY2025, connections increased to 15M with revenue rising to $110M, reflecting a growth rate of 25% and an additional revenue of $20M.

lines.text: In the Vodafone Group's 2Q FY2025 report, the IoT connection growth metrics indicate that in Q1 FY2025, there were 12M
connections generating revenue of $90M, while in Q2 FY2025, connections increased to 15M with revenue rising to $110M,
reflecting a growth rate of 25% and an additional revenue of $20M.

page.content: The total connections as of Q2 FY2025 reached 27M, with cumulative revenue totaling $200M, leading to an average revenue per connection of $7.40. In the ARPU trends by service, total ARPU stood at $85.6M, with a service breakdown for mobile showing $35.2M in revenue, a growth of 5.4%, and accounting for 45.3% of total users, totaling 10.5M in Q2 FY2025, compared to $30.5M, a 4.2% growth with 43.1% of total users, totaling 10M in Quarter 1. Broadband services generated $50.4M in revenue with a growth of 6.1% and represented 30.8% of total users at 8M in Q2 FY2025, up from $47.3M, a 5.8% growth with 29.5% of total users at 7.5M in Quarter 1.

lines.text: The total connections as of Q2 FY2025 reached 27M, with cumulative revenue totaling $200M, leading to an average revenue
per connection of $7.40. In the ARPU trends by service, total ARPU stood at $85.6M, with a service breakdown for mobile
showing $35.2M in revenue, a growth of 5.4%, and accounting for 45.3% of total users, totaling 10.5M in Q2 FY2025,

$50.4M in revenue with a growth of 6.1% and represented 30.8% of total users at 8M in Q2 FY2025, up from $47.3M, a 5.8%
growth with 29.5% of total users at 7.5M in Quarter 1.

page.content: The total number of users across both services reached 18.5M.

lines.text: The total number of users across both services reached 18.5M.
